title: What godly self-control actually means (it's not suppression)
caption:
Most men who are trying to have self-control are suppressing themselves. Holding down what's pushing up. White-knuckling through the temptation, hoping time fixes it.

That's not what Galatians 5 describes. Self-control in Scripture is a fruit, not a discipline. It grows from the Spirit. The man connected to God doesn't suppress his desires by willpower. He's been given a different set of desires. The root produces the fruit.

Governance comes from having a higher king. A man submitted to Christ doesn't fight his impulses alone. He's under authority. And a man under authority can say no to anything that conflicts with that authority. Not because he is strong. Because he is loyal.

He doesn't negotiate with the temptation. He already decided before it arrived.

What are you still white-knuckling that you could surrender instead?

Save this and send it to a man who's tired of trying harder.

#faith #scripture #christianmen #selfcontrol #godlymen #manofgod #galatians #christianity #discipline
